text:
There once was a kid named Jake who could talk to animals. Whenever a cat came into his room, he'd have a conversation about books.

Jake's favorite time of day was after dusk, when nocturnal creatures were active. He liked to think they listened to him about famous writers.

One day, while playing quietly near his bedroom window, Jake noticed something sparkling. It was a tiny butterfly with wings like fabric.

He approached, and the butterfly landed on his shoulder. "Hi there," Jake said.

The butterfly fluttered. "Hi."

"I'm Jake."

"Howdy, Jake."

Jake talked to the butterfly about his favorite books. It told him a soft wing story about a wise owl who loved ancient tales. Jake learned that courage might look like just a wing flap.

That evening, he dreamed of a forest whispering legends under moonlit trees. By morning light, he could remember the owl's wisdom. He felt more bold.

Jake decided to write his own story. It was
